subject: A Review of Set Theory
question: Find the complement of $A = \{1, 2, 3\}$ if $U = \{1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6\}$.
explanation: From the definition, $A^c = \{4, 5, 6\}$ The relative complement of A with respect to B (See Figure 2.1(II)) is the set $B - A = \{x \in U \mid x \in B \text{ and } x \notin A\}$. Figure 2.1
